relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Ephesus is addressed by the One walking among lampstands.
Endurance and discernment are seen.
They have borne much without growing weary.
But first love has been left.
Remember, turn, and do the first works.
False works are resisted.
The conqueror eats from the tree of life.
Smyrna hears from the One who died and lived.
Poverty under pressure is seen as richness.
Do not fear; faithfulness receives crown of life.
The second death does not harm the conqueror.
Pergamum hears the word-sword.
They hold the name amid hostile power.
Compromise with stumbling is named.
False teaching remains present.
Turn before the word contends.
Hidden manna and a secret name are promised.
Thyatira sees the fire-eyed Son.
Love, trust, service, and endurance are seen.
Misleading teaching is confronted.
Time to turn was given.
Harmful union becomes pressure until turning.
The heart-searching One gives according to works.
The faithful rest are not given extra weight.
Hold fast until he comes.
The conqueror shares shepherding authority.
Iron-rod imagery marks decisive rule, not personal cruelty.
The morning star is given.
Hear what Spirit says to the assemblies.

contemplative_note: Revelation 2 is lampstand diagnosis: love must not be abandoned, pressure must not create fear, and discernment must not become cruelty.
